topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The music was a vibrant, swelling symphony, the kind that makes you want to move, but my focus was entirely on the meticulously arranged floral centerpiece across the room. Then, a familiar silhouette caught my eye near the champagne fountain. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that felt like it snagged in my throat. It was him. Mark. He was laughing, his arm draped casually around a woman whose dress shimmered under the ballroom lights. My hand, mid-sip of lukewarm sparkling water, froze halfway to my lips. The noise of the party seemed to recede, replaced by a sudden, deafening silence in my own head. I gripped my glass so tightly my knuckles turned white, my vision momentarily blurring at the edges. Every muscle in my body tightened, a strange, cold pressure spreading from my chest outward. I slowly lowered the glass, the clink of glass against crystal sounding unnaturally loud. I felt a distinct, dizzying lurch, a complete disruption of my equilibrium.

[story 2]

I was navigating the crowded reception, trying to keep a polite distance from the boisterous energy, when I bumped squarely into someone. The impact was minor, a slight jostle, but it was enough to send a jolt straight through me. I looked up, and the world narrowed to that single point of recognition. It was Sarah. My ex. She was standing right there, smiling warmly at the group, her hair catching the light in a way I hadn't seen in years. My carefully constructed composure evaporated. My mouth went dry, and I could feel a peculiar heat rising up my neck. I stopped breathing for a beat, my eyes wide, darting around the room as if searching for an escape route. I mumbled something incoherent, a sound that felt too high-pitched, and quickly pivoted, walking away with a speed that bordered on frantic, desperate to put physical space between us.

[story 3]

The atmosphere in the venue was electric, a heady mix of celebration and forced conviviality. I was chatting with a mutual acquaintance near the buffet when I turned a corner and came face-to-face with him. It was him. Unmistakably him. He looked up, and for a split second, the ambient noise of the celebration seemed to vanish. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic, irregular rhythm. I felt a strange hollowness open up in my stomach, a sudden, profound sense of disorientation. I managed a tight, strained smile that felt entirely foreign on my face, my gaze locked on his. I took an involuntary step backward, my feet feeling suddenly unsteady on the polished floor, my entire posture rigid as I tried to process the reality of the situation unfolding before me.
